title: Chicken Pad Thai
description: A restaurant-quality Pad Thai you can make at home with everyday supermarket ingredients. Tender chicken, chewy rice noodles, and crunchy beansprouts tossed in a tangy-sweet tamarind sauce with scrambled eggs, tofu, and crushed peanuts.
ingredients: 125 g dried rice stick noodles (pad thai width)
150 g chicken breast or thigh, thinly sliced
1 1/2 cups beansprouts, plus extra for serving
1/2 cup firm tofu, cut into 3cm batons
1/4 cup garlic chives, cut into 3cm pieces
Ground chilli or cayenne pepper, optional
instructions: Place 125 g rice stick noodles in a large bowl and pour over plenty of boiling water. Soak for 5 minutes, then drain in a colander and quickly rinse under cold water. Do not leave them sitting for more than 5-10 minutes.
In a small bowl, mix the sauce: combine 1 1/2 tbsp tamarind puree, 3 tbsp packed brown sugar, 2 tbsp fish sauce, and 1 1/2 tbsp oyster sauce. Stir until the sugar dissolves and set aside.
Heat 2 tbsp oil in a large non-stick pan or well-seasoned wok over high heat. Add 2 finely chopped garlic cloves and 1/2 sliced onion and cook for 30 seconds.
Add 150 g thinly sliced chicken and cook for 1 1/2 minutes until mostly cooked through.
Push the chicken to one side of the pan. Pour 2 whisked eggs onto the other side. Scramble with a wooden spoon (add a touch of extra oil if the pan is too dry), then mix the eggs into the chicken.
Add 1 1/2 cups beansprouts, 1/2 cup tofu batons, the drained noodles, and the prepared sauce to the pan.
Toss gently for about 1 1/2 minutes until the sauce is absorbed by the noodles.
Add 1/4 cup garlic chives and half the chopped peanuts. Toss through quickly, then remove from heat.
Serve immediately, sprinkled with the remaining peanuts and lime wedges on the side. Add a sprinkle of chilli and extra beansprouts if desired. Squeeze lime juice over the top to taste before eating.
